patches.replace: only a relabelling of it). A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a parameterless surface fit to observed pole magnitudes (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1), the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no predictive work.
patches.rationale: EXP-219: Add third horn to forced disjunction — the metaphysical/aetheric retreat that waives ∇·B = 0 abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on, demoting B(r) to a surface fit (§6.1 cross-ref).

patches.find: Globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay.
patches.replace: The dome model asserts that globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay.
patches.rationale: WIN-004 c4 hole 2 (minor): Prefix claim with attribution to avoid reading as our own concession.

patches.find: The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism for flux-patch separation.
patches.replace:
The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism for flux-patch separation.

The mechanism gap is asymmetric: globe-model MHD provides reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary and westward-drift advection from core flow — deriving directional asymmetry from known physical processes. The dome labels the same phenomenon 'aetheric vortex decay' with no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry and no published functional form (50.6° is hardcoded; the exponential rate parameter τ is unpublished). Acknowledging the bifurcation rate as observational does not reduce our credibility — both sides use the same number, and the standard model explains the mechanism while the dome does not.
patches.rationale: WIN-004 c4 hole 1 (moderate): Add asymmetric mechanism paragraph to detail_verdict_text to preempt defender pivot that our conceding the observational rate weakens the argument.

patches.find: Either way, the model's own height function is off by two to three orders of magnitude.
patches.replace: Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation). This is not a matter of calibration or measurement uncertainty; it is a qualitative failure of the dome's gravity mechanism.
patches.rationale: WIN-067 c8 hole 1 (major): Carry-forward c7 H1 — make the 170–1,430× range explicit in the closing sentence instead of vague "two to three orders of magnitude" so the range is surfaced in the paragraph conclusion.
WIN-067 c8 hole 2 (minor): Arithmetic precision fix — use 0.0062 rather than ~0.006 as the apples-to-apples value.

patches.find: the author's engineering implicitly agrees the theological claim is untestable.
patches.replace: the author's engineering implicitly agrees the theological claim is untestable. Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's confirmed-prediction scorecard alongside empirically testable WINs — the scorecard accuracy figure does not distinguish between testable predictions and unfalsifiable theological reframings.
patches.rationale: WIN-031 c5 hole 1 (moderate): Add scorecard-accounting critique to detail_verdict_text to match finding/evidence/tldr_verdict which already carry this argument.

patches.find: not the pole-apex of a dome disc.
patches.replace: not the pole-apex of a dome disc. This cartographic convergence is expected: any culture located in the Levant will mythologize a northern mountain as cosmically significant, but this is a consequence of geographic orientation, not dome geometry.
patches.rationale: WIN-031 c5 hole 2 (moderate): Add preemptive clause after cartographic direction sentence to close the defender pivot that our acknowledgment of Mount Zaphon supports the dome.
